name: Bean and Rice Burgers
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 cups Cooked beans (e.g., pinto, kidney, black)
1/2 cup Wheat flour (or all-purpose white flour)
1 tablespoon Seasoning salt (sodium chloride and other spices as desired)
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat a greased grill or electric frying pan on medium heat. Alternatively, grease a 13" x 9-inch glass baking pan and bake at 350°F for 30 minutes.
2. Spread the mixture in the prepared pan and cook uncovered. Use a pancake turner to cut and remove cooked "burgers."
3. Mash the beans.
4. Add all ingredients and mix well. If the mixture is too dry, you may need to add 1/4 to 1/2 cup of soy milk, nut milk, rice milk, or whole milk. Do not thin it excessively, or it will become mushy.
5. Form about 2 heaping tablespoons of mixture onto the grill or frying pan for each burger and flatten with a greased pancake turner.
6. Turn several times rather than just once on each side (as you would for conventional burgers) to achieve better texture.
